questions.question: Which HTML element is used to write CSS rules directly within an HTML document?
questions.explanation: The <style> element allows you to write CSS rules directly inside the HTML document, typically placed in the <head> section.

questions.question: Which method of applying CSS has the highest specificity?
questions.explanation: Inline styles applied via the style attribute have the highest specificity and override both internal and external stylesheet rules (unless !important is used).

questions.question: What is the correct HTML attribute for applying inline CSS to an element?
questions.explanation:
The style attribute is used to apply inline CSS directly to an HTML element, e.g.,

.

questions.question: Why are external stylesheets generally preferred over inline styles?
choices: They allow separation of concerns and reuse across pages
questions.explanation: External stylesheets separate structure (HTML) from presentation (CSS), making code more maintainable and allowing styles to be reused across multiple pages.

questions.question: The ___ attribute is used to apply CSS styles directly to a single HTML element.
questions.explanation: The style attribute accepts CSS property-value pairs and applies them directly to the element, e.g., style="color: blue; font-size: 14px;".

questions.question: CSS rules defined inside a <style> tag in the HTML document are called ___ styles.
questions.explanation: Internal (or embedded) styles are CSS rules written inside a <style> element within the HTML document, as opposed to external stylesheets linked via <link>.
